mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and driving S&P 500 returns down over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary policy tightening, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50 percent of cases when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy, an increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output that translates immediately into higher short-term equity prices as investors price in improved capacity utilization.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of observations during easing periods, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS declines by less than 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts signal increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which boosts related sector earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with housing starts growth (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity multiples and resulting in negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of threshold-crossing events, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear markets with a t-statistic above 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a negative out-of-sample R-squared, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52 percent of cases when both labor indicators move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: During economic contractions, a decline in real GDP growth reflects shrinking corporate profit margins and reduced investment, which systematically depresses S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of recessionary observations, or the signal fails to predict downturns with an accuracy exceeding 60 percent in walk-forward validation.
